pr.title: Cleanup statics in vi, match and link vi1202
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Split the crowd sound-effect manager code out of src/melee/vi/vi1202.c into a new src/melee/sfx/crowdsfx.c/.h unit, updated splits/configure/symbols so vi1202 became Matching, and cleaned duplicated static JObj setter helpers in the vi files by using existing WithMtxDirty helpers and a small vi1202_SetupChild helper. The PR author explicitly said matching vi1202 required introducing a logically separate crowdsfx.c split.
what_changed: Changed config/GALE01/splits.txt so melee/vi/vi1202.c ends at text 0x80321900 instead of 0x80322620, and added a new melee/sfx/crowdsfx.c split covering text 0x80321900-0x80322620 plus the moved .bss/.sbss/.sdata2 ranges.
Changed configure.py to mark Object(Matching, "melee/vi/vi1202.c") instead of NonMatching, and added a new MeleeLib section for "sfx (Sound effects)" with Object(NonMatching, "melee/sfx/crowdsfx.c").

Moved the large crowd/gasp/cheer sound-effect manager block from vi1202.c into new src/melee/sfx/crowdsfx.c, including un_80321900 through un_80322598, CrowdSFX_UnkStruct state, gCrowdConfig use, lbAudioAx calls, fighter/player checks, and mpLib blast-zone accesses.
Added src/melee/sfx/crowdsfx.h with CrowdSFX_UnkStruct, CrowdConfig, gCrowdConfig, and public prototypes for the un_80321900-range crowd SFX functions.
Replaced vi1202.h includes in fighter and game-mode call sites with sfx/crowdsfx.h because those users depended on the moved crowd SFX declarations rather than vi1202 scene declarations.
Cleaned vi1202.c by removing the embedded vi1202_UnkStruct/crowd globals and crowd functions, removing redundant ft/pl includes, and adding vi1202_SetupChild to wrap child JObj transform setup.

Removed local duplicated static HSD_JObjSetRotationY_2, HSD_JObjSetScale*_2, and HSD_JObjSetTranslate*_2 helper copies in vi1201v1.c, vi1201v2.c, and vi1202.c, replacing the vi1202 call path with HSD_JObjSet*WithMtxDirty helpers.
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt so vi1202-local string/float symbols became local @216-@321-style symbols rather than global un_/lbl_ names where appropriate.
smart_moves: Used a file split instead of forcing unrelated crowd SFX logic to remain in vi1202; this preserved a more logical subsystem boundary and made vi1202 itself match/link cleanly.
Kept external users honest by switching includes from vi/vi1202.h to sfx/crowdsfx.h in fighter and gm files, reducing accidental coupling to the vi scene file.

Recorded naming evidence directly in crowdsfx.h: the header comments cite UnclePunch Training Mode map names, lbAudio_PlayCrowdSFX, and observed crowd gasp/cheer behavior as evidence for the Crowd/CrowdSFX terminology.
Used local symbol scope for compiler-generated or file-local constants in symbols.txt instead of leaving them as misleading global un_/lbl_ symbols.
Abstracted repeated vi1202 child transform setup into vi1202_SetupChild while relying on existing HSD_JObjSet*WithMtxDirty helper names rather than maintaining duplicated inline static setter bodies.
decomp_lessons: When a range inside an existing file is functionally a distinct subsystem, a new split can be the cleanest way to both match code and improve source organization.
After splitting code, update all three axes together: splits.txt address ranges, configure.py object/library membership, and include/header ownership at call sites.
Do not leave declarations in the old owner header just to avoid include churn; move declarations to the subsystem header and update dependents.

Duplicated local versions of common baselib setters can often be removed in favor of established project helper wrappers, but stack padding and helper extraction may need adjustment to preserve matching.
Use comments in new headers to document why an otherwise uncertain subsystem name is being adopted, especially when evidence comes from external symbol maps or gameplay behavior.

Moving code between objects may look like broken matches in the old unit and new matches in the new unit; interpret decomp.dev reports in terms of the intended split boundary, not just per-old-unit regressions.
naming_conventions: New subsystem/file name: sfx/crowdsfx.c and sfx/crowdsfx.h for crowd gasp/cheer sound effects.
New types use descriptive Crowd-prefixed names: CrowdSFX_UnkStruct for the still-unknown manager state and CrowdConfig for configuration data.
Known-but-still-unidentified functions retained address-based un_8032xxxx names, while a per-frame callback kept fn_803219AC naming.
Compiler/local constants in symbols.txt were renamed to local @216, @217, @218, etc. instead of global un_804D5B30/un_804D5B38/un_804003A0-style names.
The new header guard was MELEE_CROWD_CROWDSFX_H, despite the file living under melee/sfx; this is evidence from the diff, not necessarily a reusable convention.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed vi1202 from NonMatching to Matching after splitting out the 0x80321900-0x80322620 crowd SFX range.
Used a new object boundary at 0x80321900 so functions un_80321900 through un_80322598 belong to melee/sfx/crowdsfx instead of melee/vi/vi1202.
Moved .bss 0x804A2F08-0x804A2F38, .sbss 0x804D7050-0x804D7054, and .sdata2 0x804DE148-0x804DE160 to the new crowdsfx split while leaving earlier vi1202 sections in vi1202.

Kept matching-sensitive constructs in crowdsfx.c such as #pragma dont_inline around un_80321C70/un_80321CA4, static inline/caller helper variants, goto labels, M2C_FIELD accesses, and PAD_STACK where needed.
Reduced vi1202 OnEnter stack padding from PAD_STACK(24) to PAD_STACK(16) after moving transform setup into vi1202_SetupChild.
Replaced hand-written HSD_JObjSet*_2 copies with HSD_JObjSetTranslateXWithMtxDirty, HSD_JObjSetRotationYWithMtxDirty, and HSD_JObjSetScale*WithMtxDirty calls in the vi1202 setup path.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.

The decomp.dev bot reported linked code increased to 42.41% (+0.05%, +2036 bytes), linked data increased to 35.01% (+0.02%, +192 bytes), and matched data slightly decreased to 41.43% (-0.01%, -144 bytes).

The bot listed 25 new matches under main/melee/sfx/crowdsfx, including un_8032233C, un_80321D30, un_80321EBC, un_8032201C, un_80321AF4, un_80321A00, un_80322178, un_803224DC, un_80322598, .bss, and .sbss.
The bot also listed the same functions as broken under main/melee/vi/vi1202, which is consistent with the deliberate split rather than evidence of a functional regression.
The report noted vi1202 .sdata2 improved from 66.67% to 100% and vi1201v2 un_80320A40_OnEnter improved slightly from 97.41% to 97.68%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename un_80321900-range crowd SFX functions or resolve CrowdSFX_UnkStruct fields.
Search for subsequent changes to gCrowdConfig or CrowdConfig field names to validate the header comments and thresholds.
Check whether the MELEE_CROWD_CROWDSFX_H guard was later normalized to an sfx-based guard.
Look for PRs that apply the same split-boundary tactic to other vi files or sound-effect manager code.
Search for later cleanup of vi1201v1/vi1201v2 JObj setter statics and remaining nonmatching percentages.
